messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Left truncated and right censored data
messages.subject_clean: Left truncated and right censored data
messages.body_plain:
I have a question concerning a non parametric estimator for left truncated
and right censored data. I see that the function


survfit(Surv(time1, time2, status) ~ group, data=mydata)

  (where time1= left truncation time
   time2= follow=up time
   status= 0:censored 1:not censored)

do this, but what's the method used? Is it a Lynden-Bell (Woodroofe)
estimator or something else?
Regards
Martina Gandini

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Left truncated and right censored data
messages.subject_clean: Left truncated and right censored data
messages.body_plain:
On Fri, 11 Nov 2005, Martina Gandini wrote:

> I have a question concerning a non parametric estimator for left truncated
> and right censored data. I see that the function
>
>
> survfit(Surv(time1, time2, status) ~ group, data=mydata)
>
>  (where time1= left truncation time
>   time2= follow=up time
>   status= 0:censored 1:not censored)
>
> do this, but what's the method used? Is it a Lynden-Bell (Woodroofe)
> estimator or something else?

I don't know what names it is called, but it is the cumulative product of
    1 - number of events/number at risk
over time points.  This is the nonparametric MLE, and reduces to the 
Kaplan-Meier estimator in the absence of truncation.

 	-thomas

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] multiple "stairs steps" plots
messages.body_plain:
You are looking for plot types "s" and "S", try for example:

x<-1:10 
y<-rnorm(10)

plot( x, y, pch=16 )
lines(x,y,type="s")

plot( x, y, pch=16 )
lines(x,y,type="S")

----------------------
Bendix Carstensen
Senior Statistician
Steno Diabetes Center

DK-2820 Gentofte
Denmark

messages.body_snippet:
You are looking for plot types "s" and "S", try for example:

x<-1:10 
y<-rnorm(10)

plot( x, y, pch=16 )
lines(x,y,type="s")

plot( x, y, pch=16 )
lines(x,y,type="S")

----------------------
Bendix C
